data.title: The Class 8 trucking recovery accelerates in February
data.description:
North American Class 8 truck/tractor preliminary net orders shot up 47% month over month in February and 159% year over year to 47,200 units. The tally was the highest for industry since September 2022 and marked the third straight month of +20% Y/Y order growth. FTR noted that while the on-highway market constituted the bulk of the increase, both on-highway and vocational markets contributed significantly to the growth in orders. 

The 2026 order season (September 2025-February 2026) is now up slightly at 4% Y/Y growth, a notable improvement from the double-digit declines earlier in the cycle. The improvement in recent months and strengthening freight conditions are seen as indications that the market is stabilizing and transitioning into the early stages of a cyclical recovery.

"February’s very solid year-over-year increase in net orders extended the firmer tone that has been building since late last year. Freight volumes and utilization are trending higher, and FTR’s rate forecasts have strengthened," highlighted FTR analyst Dan Moyer.

Looking ahead, risks seen for the durability of the freight recovery include still-high financing costs, the potential for tariff or regulatory shifts, and geopolitical risks such as the new conflict in the Middle East.

Select trucking, freight, and logistics stocks: PACCAR (PCAR), Daimler Truck (DTRUY), Traton (TRATF), Volvo Group (VLVLY), Cummins (CMI), Knight-Swift Transportation (KNX), Marten Transport (MRTN), Old Dominion Freight Line (ODFL), U-Haul Holding (UHAL), Landstar System (LSTR), Werner Enterprises (WERN), XPO (XPO), RXO (RXO), Universal Logistics Holdings (ULH), Convenent Logistics Group (CVG), Schneider National (SNDR), J.B. Hunt Transport Services (JBHT), Heartland Express (HTLD), Saia (SAIA), UPS (UPS), FedEx (FDX) and ArcBest (ARCB).

data.title: Cresco Labs reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Cresco Labs press release (CRLBF): Q4 Revenue of $161.55M (-8.2% Y/Y) beats by $0.32M.
  • Gross profit of $83 million. Adjusted gross profit1 of $84 million; and an Adjusted gross margin1 of 52.2%.
  • SG&A of $57 million or 35.3% of revenue.
  • Net loss of $89 million, which includes one-time, non-cash charges of $93 million related to the write-down of the New York reporting unit.
  • Fourth quarter Adjusted EBITDA1 of $40 million and Adjusted EBITDA margin1 of 25.0%.

data.title: Dave prices $175M convertible debt offering
data.description:
Dave (DAVE) said it priced a $175M offering of 0% convertible senior notes due 2031 in a private placement to institutional investors. The company also gave buyers the option to purchase up to $25M more in notes within 13 days.

The offering was increased from the previously announced $150M, and the deal is expected to close March 9, 2026, subject to customary conditions.

The notes will not bear regular interest, and the principal amount of the notes will not accrete. The notes will mature on April 1, 2031, unless earlier converted, redeemed, or repurchased.

The initial conversion rate is 3.5825 shares of common stock per $1,000 principal amount of the notes, which is equivalent to ~$279.13 per share. The initial conversion price represents a premium of around 32.5% over the last reported sale price of $210.67 per share on March 4, 2026.

Dave (DAVE) estimates that the net proceeds from the offering will be ~$168M, or ~$192.1M if the initial purchasers exercise in full their option to purchase additional notes. The firm plans to use the proceeds to fund the approximate $15.1M cost of the capped call transactions, to repurchase around 334,000 shares using ~$70.5M of the net proceeds, and for general corporate purposes.

data.title: EPAM enters $300M accelerated share repurchase agreement
data.description:
• EPAM Systems (EPAM) Thursday announced that it has entered into an accelerated share repurchase agreement with Morgan Stanley (MS) to repurchase an aggregate of $300 million of its common stock. 
  • The ASR was authorized under the company's existing $1 billion share repurchase authorization.
  • Under the terms of the ASR, the company will make a payment of $300 million to Morgan Stanley and receive from Morgan Stanley an initial share delivery of 1,703,336 shares, or $240 million worth based on the closing price on March 4, 2026.

data.title: Decline in JD.com's core unit, surging costs weigh on Q4 results
data.description:
Chinese online marketplace operator JD.com (JD) (JDCMF) posted a profit miss and inline revenue for the fourth quarter on Thursday, hurt by a decline in its core e-commerce segment and higher operating expenses.

Shares of the company are down 1.2% in premarket trading and are down more than 10% on the OTC Exchange.

During Q4, revenue in JD Retail fell 1.7% to RMB 301.9B, JD Logistics was up 21.9% to RMB 63.53B, and the new business segment saw a three-fold rise to RMB 14.09B.

Total expenses breakdown: Fulfillment expenses rose by 20.7% to RMB 24.3B; marketing expenses rose by 50.6% to RMB 25.3B; R&D expenses rose by 52% to RMB 6.7B; G&A expenses rose by 34.8% to RMB 3.3B. 

Operating margin for Q4 was a negative 1.7% compared to 2.4% growth for last year. 

Net loss attributable to the company’s ordinary shareholders was RMB 2.7B, or RMB 2.07 loss per share, compared with a net income of RMB 9.9B, or RMB 6.47 earnings per share, for the same period last year.

On an adjusted per-share basis, the Beijing-based company earned RMB 0.57, missing the average analyst estimate of RMB 0.70 polled by FactSet.

Revenue in Q4 was RMB 352.28B, which is comparable to the RMB 351.58B estimate. However, revenue growth was 1.5% vs. 13.4% last year.

data.title: Most and least shorted stocks over $2B market cap
data.description:
Short sellers are heavily targeting several mid-to-large-cap stocks above the $2B market cap threshold, led by Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) with 36.9% short interest, while names such as The Bank of N.T. Butterfield & Son Limited (NTB) and Eli Lilly and Company (LLY) remain among the least shorted, with short interest below 1%.

Here’s a list of the top 10 most shorted mid- to mega-cap stocks (as a % of shares outstanding):

  • Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) – 36.90%
  • Acadia Healthcare Company (ACHC) – 35.19%
  • SoundHound AI (SOUN) – 34.57%
  • CleanSpark (CLSK) – 33.42%
  • Ondas (ONDS) – 33.23%
  • MARA Holdings (MARA) – 29.19%
  • Applied Digital (APLD) – 28.73%
  • NETSTREIT (NTST) – 28.63%
  • Sphere Entertainment (SPHR) – 26.14%
  • Eos Energy Enterprises (EOSE) – 26.14%

Here’s a list of the 10 least shorted mid-to-mega cap stocks (short interest 0.5% and above):

  • The Bank of N.T. Butterfield & Son (NTB) – 0.58%
  • Grupo Aeroméxico, S.A.B. de C.V. (AERO) – 0.60%
  • HEICO (HEI.A) – 0.63%
  • Ardagh Metal Packaging (AMBP) – 0.63%
  • CNA Financial (CNA) – 0.64%
  • Twenty One Capital (XXI) – 0.64%
  • EverCommerce (EVCM) – 0.65%
  • MINISO Group Holding (MNSO) – 0.71%
  • SOLV Energy (MWH) – 0.71%
  • Eli Lilly (LLY) – 0.71%

Broad-market ETFs such as (SPY), (QQQ), and (IWM) track some of these names.
